title: Profiling, Event-Loop Health, Memory, Load, and Capacity · Narrowing Workshop
summary: A backend pairing room predicts runtime, request, middleware, dependency, response, and cleanup transitions before assembling the first performance experiment and capacity envelope for profiling, event-loop health, memory, load, and capacity. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of performance experiment and capacity envelope while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence def…
objectives: Translate user latency and throughput outcomes into request-path budgets, resource hypotheses, experiment controls, and stop conditions before optimizing.
Use CPU profiles, heap snapshots, allocation, garbage collection, event-loop delay, handles, and request traces to locate causal bottlenecks safely.
Model arrival rate, concurrency, payload, endpoint mix, warmup, caches, connection reuse, percentiles, coordinated omission, errors, and saturation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Commit to the mission model · Workshop Performance Hypothesis Budget
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop team handles case 6777 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to gate tail latency with event-loop and utilization capacity evidence; preserve one tenant fairness invariant; then fail the canary while the old revision drains and prove rollback remains safe. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Translate user latency and throughput outcomes into request-path budgets, resource hypotheses, experiment controls, and stop conditions before optimizing.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Node and Express server Commit to the mission model · Workshop Performance Hypothesis Budget.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for translate user latency and throughput outcomes into request-path budgets, resource hypotheses, experiment controls, and stop conditions before optimizing. For Node and Express server Commit to the mission model · Workshop Performance Hypothesis Budget, keep Performance Hypothesis Budget connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.

lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Gate changed builds with noise-aware baselines, representative cases, resource ceilings, overload recovery, rollback thresholds, and retained profiles.

options.text: Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder. That claim does not survive the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary.

For test an acceptance boundary, run one convenient Performance Regression Recovery happy path, omit method, authority, credential, byte, time, redirect, retry, and cleanup boundaries, then accept plausible output.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Performance Regression Recovery condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.

steps.title: Node and Express server Defend the transferred model · Workshop Performance Regression Recovery
steps.instruction:
Write the workshop evidence note for Performance Regression Recovery: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A workshop team handles case 2252 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to gate tail latency with event-loop and utilization capacity evidence; preserve one tenant fairness invariant; then saturate one tenant budget while another tenant remains serviceable. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Gate changed builds with noise-aware baselines, representative cases, resource ceilings, overload recovery, rollback thresholds, and retained profiles.

Evidence target: Node and Express server Defend the transferred model · Workshop Performance Regression Recovery.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Node and Express server Defend the transferred model · Workshop Performance Regression Recovery, keep Performance Regression Recovery connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Gate changed builds with noise-aware baselines, representative cases, resource ceilings, overload recovery, rollback thresholds, and retained profiles.

hints: For Node and Express server Defend the transferred model · Workshop Performance Regression Recovery, name the method, authority, body, byte, time, and credential boundary, Performance Regression Recovery precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Node and Express server Defend the transferred model · Workshop Performance Regression Recovery, trace this concrete input and predict resolution, redirect, status, representation, retry, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: A workshop team handles case 2252 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to gate tail latency with event-loop and utilization capacity evidence; preserve one tenant fairness invariant; then saturate one tenant budget while another tenant remains serviceable. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Gate changed builds with noise-aware baselines, representative cases, resource ceilings, overload recovery, rollback thresholds, and retained profiles.

For Node and Express server Defend the transferred model · Workshop Performance Regression Recovery, challenge the documented misconception with request, response, fault, policy, and transfer evidence: One faster benchmark run proves a performance regression has been fixed.
